title: Appropriate memory de-allocation should be preferred
description: The same form that was used to create an object should always be used to delete it. Specifically, deallocation should correspond to allocation as per the table below. Table 1.
recommendation: Refactor to eliminate the flagged pattern.

noncompliant:
int* p1 = new int[10];
delete p1; // Noncompliant: should use delete[]

int* p2 = new int;
delete[] p2; // Noncompliant: should use delete

int* p3 = (int*)malloc(sizeof(int) * 10);
delete[] p3; // Noncompliant: should use free()
compliant:
int* p1 = new int[10];
delete[] p1; // Compliant: correct deallocation for array

int* p2 = new int;
delete p2; // Compliant: correct deallocation for single object

int* p3 = (int*)malloc(sizeof(int) * 10);
free(p3); // Compliant: correct deallocation for malloc

checks.message: Inappropriate memory deallocation: {allocation_type} should be paired with {correct_deallocation}
